title: Quiz: Resource Management for Quantum Computing

questions.question: In the QPUAllocator's 'best fit' allocation strategy, what factors are considered when scoring a QPU for a job?

options.text: Only the number of qubits available
Qubit efficiency, estimated fidelity, queue time, and cost
Only the cost per shot
Only the queue time estimate

questions.explanation: The best-fit strategy considers multiple factors: qubit efficiency (how closely the QPU size matches requirements), estimated circuit fidelity, queue time penalty, and cost efficiency. This holistic approach ensures the most suitable QPU is selected.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'starvation prevention' mechanism in the QuantumJobQueue?

options.text: To ensure jobs with high shots counts execute faster
To promote low-priority jobs that have been waiting too long
To cancel jobs that exceed their deadline
To reduce the cost of quantum execution

questions.explanation: Starvation prevention automatically promotes low-priority jobs that have been waiting longer than the fairness window to a higher priority queue, ensuring they eventually get executed even under heavy load from high-priority jobs.

questions.question: In the CostOptimizer, what is the primary benefit of 'shot batching'?

options.text: It reduces circuit depth
It improves measurement fidelity
It helps reach volume discount thresholds for lower per-shot costs
It reduces queue wait times

questions.explanation: Shot batching groups multiple jobs together to reach volume discount thresholds, reducing the effective cost per shot. Quantum cloud providers often offer significant discounts for larger batch executions.

questions.question: What distinguishes the 'adaptive' load balancing strategy from 'round-robin' in the MultiBackendManager?

options.text: Adaptive uses random selection while round-robin is sequential
Adaptive considers health scores, current load, weights, and job requirements
Adaptive only works with a single backend
Adaptive is faster but less fair

questions.explanation: The adaptive strategy dynamically scores backends based on multiple factors including health scores, current connection load, configured weights, and job-specific requirements. This provides smarter routing compared to simple round-robin.

questions.question: In the QuantumResourcePool, what happens when a resource exceeds the configured timeout while still allocated?

options.text: The job using it is cancelled
An alert is sent to the administrator
The resource is automatically released back to the pool
The pool creates a new resource

questions.explanation: The cleanup mechanism automatically releases resources that have exceeded the timeout duration, returning them to the pool for other users. This prevents resource hoarding and ensures pool availability.

questions.question: When using 'execute_with_failover' in MultiBackendManager, what happens when execution fails on the selected backend?

options.text: The job is immediately marked as failed
The manager retries on a different backend that hasn't been tried yet
The same backend is retried after a delay
All backends are tried simultaneously

questions.explanation: The failover mechanism maintains a set of attempted backends and selects a different healthy backend that hasn't been tried yet. This continues until success or all backends have been exhausted.

questions.question: In the 'weighted_round_robin' load balancing strategy, backends with higher weights receive proportionally more job assignments.
questions.explanation: True. The weighted round-robin strategy uses probabilistic selection where the probability of selecting a backend is proportional to its weight. A backend with weight 2.0 will receive approximately twice as many jobs as one with weight 1.0.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid cost optimization strategies for quantum computing? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Batching multiple jobs to reach volume discount thresholds
Lowering job priority to reduce per-shot costs
Using reserved capacity for predictable workloads
Increasing circuit depth to reduce shots needed
Selecting backends based on cost-per-shot rates
Time-shifting jobs to off-peak pricing windows

questions.explanation: Valid cost optimization strategies include batching for volume discounts, lowering priority for cheaper rates, reserved capacity for discounts, backend selection based on cost, and time-shifting to cheaper windows. Increasing circuit depth doesn't reduce costs and typically reduces result quality.
